titleStmt.title: Letter from Wilfrid Spender to Lillian Spender, 10 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender (1876 - 1960) ti his wife, Lillian (1880-1968). Spender was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender wrote a collection of letters to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War."

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland
msIdentifier.collection: Letters from Sir Wilfrid Spender to Lady Spender, D1633/1/1/95

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Dec. 10th My own pr a very few hurried lines as I have a big
job on hand in the way of indexing for the General. Why isn't
a p r here to help? Yes I think Douglas & M'Gill would appreciate cakes as
much as anything but you are hardly likely to be able to
Xmas time it exactly. The latter would probably like something
gaudy. As regards Mrs KS remember the more she scratches
& the less notice you take the greater your triumph over her. I have been thinking things over & have settled that
as the p r is now manager of our affairs she ought
to get the manager's fees. Consequently whatever we put
into the war loan during the war I will put in to
your name & I hope to be able to wash out the extrava-
gance fund ( & a good deal more if Rubber doesn't bust !) The PR who I admit needs no other stimulus than
the B W's approval will now know that she will get
some little benefit herself as well, not that it now
makes much difference. I had a walk today after 2 days without much
exercise owing to Russell wanting me to go with
him. Tomorrow I am to dine with Indian Corps, &
I had an invite to dine with the Arty VII
Dir but declined it they are 14 M off & tho' I
should have liked to have seen Lindsellagain I
find I cannot gallivant now. Must end but much love Your b w Wilfrid BSpender I want to hear how all at 24 are now that
they have a p r to play with
